Benchmarks complete. Here are the results comparing **.NET 9** vs **.NET 10**:

| Method | .NET 9.0 | .NET 10.0 | Change |
|--------|----------|-----------|--------|
| **AverageWordLength** | 9.578 μs | 8.058 μs | ~16% faster |
| **WordLengthHistogram** | 15.214 μs | 5.872 μs | ~61% faster |
| **UniqueWordCount** | 20.334 μs | 41.634 μs | ~105% slower |

**Key observations:**
- **AverageWordLength** and **WordLengthHistogram** show clear improvements on .NET 10.
- **UniqueWordCount** regresses significantly on .NET 10 — though note the high StdDev (7.7 μs) suggests machine noise may be a factor (you mentioned the machine may be under load).
- Memory allocations are identical across both runtimes (0 B, 112 B, 760 B respectively).

The benchmark project is at `TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/` and full BDN artifacts are in `TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/BenchmarkDotNet.Artifacts/`.
